body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BBNI) has launched an innovation hub at Sarinah Building, aiming to drive digital transformation and create sustainable growth through innovative products like Wondr BNI, a simplified mobile banking application. Since its operation in September 2023, the hub has fostered a startup-like working atmosphere, encouraging collaboration and creative work culture among its young employees, which is expected to boost digital platform development and drive growth.
title: Bank Negara Indonesia launches innovation hub to drive digital transformation and simplify banking transactions.

body: PT Bakrieland Development Tbk (ELTY) has acquired 100% shares of PT Graha Istana Nirwana, the manager of Aston Sidoarjo City Hotel & Conference Centre, through its subsidiary PT Mutiara Masyhur Sejahtera, with a total value of IDR 93.6 billion. The acquisition aims to strengthen the company's recurring income through hotel room rentals and food beverage sales, and is expected to increase its long-term revenue contribution.
title: PT Bakrieland Development acquires 100% shares of PT Graha Istana Nirwana, operator of Aston Sidoarjo City Hotel and Conference Centre, through debt conversion mechanism.

body: PT Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO) has successfully converted herbal waste into agricultural fertilizers and livestock nutrition supplements, resulting in a 720 kg increase in rice crop yields per hectare and a 3 kg daily weight gain in cattle. The company's product, Herbafarm, has also shown positive results in reducing mortality rates and increasing production yields in laying hen and broiler quail farms, with potential for significant revenue growth.
title: PT Sido Muncul Successfully Converts Herbal Waste into Agricultural Fertilizer and Livestock Nutrition Supplements

body: Net foreign volume reached -586.11 million shares, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (TKPK) experiencing the largest net sale of 909.09 million shares, followed by PT Bumi Resources TBK (BUMI) with a net sale of 136.28 million shares. On the buying side, PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK (BIPI) saw the largest net purchase of 48.87 million shares, followed by PT MNC Land TBK (KPIG) with a net purchase of 37.24 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell majority of shares in various Indonesian companies, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK seeing the largest net sale.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) fell 0.43% or 30.70 points to 7,065.75, with a total transaction value of IDR 9.67 trillion and trading frequency of 1.02 million times. PT Agro Yasa Lestari Tbk (no ticker provided) led the top gainers with a 34.29% increase in share price, while PT Bank Artha Graha Internasional Tbk (no ticker provided) led the top losers with an 18.12% decrease.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Falls 0.43% to 7,065.75 Points Amid Mixed Sectoral Performance

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index rose 1.61% or 112.58 points to 7,096.45 on Monday, led by shares of PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), which increased 3.69% and 2.64%, respectively. The trading volume reached 19.05 billion shares, with a transaction value of IDR 9.59 trillion (USD 594 million), while foreign investors recorded a net sell of IDR 399.42 billion (USD 24.72 million).
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Rises 1.61% Led by Banking Shares

body: Foreign investors sold a net 133.50 million shares of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), 36.16 million shares of PT Surya Citra Media Tbk, and 26.43 million shares of PT Darma Henwa Tbk, among others, while buying a net 97.55 million shares of PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, 73.41 million shares of PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk, and 25.46 million shares of PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk. The transactions resulted in significant net volumes for these companies, with PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk experiencing the largest net buys.
title: Foreign investors sell majority of shares in Indonesian companies, with digital company PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK topping the list.

body: PT Waskita Beton Precast Tbk (WSBP) plans to increase its capital expenditure to IDR 50 billion in 2025, a 20 billion increase from 2024, to repair precast molds and other equipment, and targets 10-15% revenue growth to IDR 2.4 trillion. The company also aims to divest unproductive equipment and land banks, expecting IDR 5 billion and IDR 50 billion in revenue, respectively, to strengthen working capital and support its growth target in 2025.
title: PT Waskita Beton Precast to Increase Capital Expenditure to IDR 50 Billion in 2025

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) will delist 8 listed companies by July 21, 2025, due to bankruptcy, with the affected companies including PT Mas Murni Indonesia Tbk, PT Forza Land Indonesia Tbk, and PT Hanson International Tbk, among others. The delisted companies have until January 18, 2025, to submit share buyback information, with the implementation period regulated by the IDX taking place from January 20 to July 18, 2025.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange to Delist 8 Companies by 2025 Due to Bankruptcy

body: CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd increased its stake in PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ENERGY) to 16.38% by acquiring 147.23 million shares, while also reducing its stake in PT Indonesian Paradise Property Tbk (IPPRO) by selling 1.15 million shares. Meanwhile, other notable transactions included PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (Elang) purchasing 69 million shares, PT Saratoga Investama Sedaya Tbk (SRTG) acquiring 53.36 million shares of PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk (MDKA), and PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk (INTP) buying back 1.32 million of its own shares.
title: Multiple Indonesian Companies See Changes in Share Ownership Following Recent Transactions

body: Wilo (no ticker symbol), a German company specializing in modern pump technology, has partnered with the Indonesian Defense University to implement advanced pump systems across various sectors, including defense, with a focus on enhancing energy efficiency and environmental sustainability. The partnership includes a donation of modern pumps for student research purposes, aiming to enhance students' capabilities in addressing modern technological challenges and potentially leading to further collaboration opportunities between the university and industry partners.
title: Indonesian Defense University partners with German company Wilo to implement advanced pump systems and enhance energy efficiency.
